text: Job seekers targeting software engineering roles should follow the 3-hour DSA block rule: solve 2–3 problems daily on LeetCode or Codeforces. Focus on patterns: sliding window, two pointers, BFS/DFS, dynamic programming.

System design study should be scheduled for 1 hour daily. Use resources like Grokking System Design, ByteByteGo, or Designing Data-Intensive Applications. Practice designing systems out loud — mock interviews with peers.
Apply to 5–10 companies daily, not just 1–2. Use a spreadsheet to track: company name, role, date applied, status, and follow-up date. Follow up via LinkedIn after 1 week of no response.

Behavioral interview prep deserves 30 minutes every day. Prepare STAR method answers for 15 core questions: tell me about yourself, conflict resolution, leadership, failure, achievement. Record and review your answers.

Mock interviews should happen at least twice per week. Use Pramp, Interviewing.io, or peer mock sessions. Feedback on communication style and problem-solving approach is as important as getting the answer right.

Job seekers should dedicate 1 hour daily to personal project enhancement or open source contributions. A strong GitHub profile with recent activity (green squares) is a silent resume that speaks louder than a PDF.

For FAANG preparation, allocate time by topic difficulty: Arrays/Strings (2 weeks), Trees/Graphs (3 weeks), DP (3 weeks), System Design (ongoing). Do not rush past weak topics — depth beats breadth in FAANG interviews.

Network actively: send 5 connection requests daily to engineers at target companies. Engage with their posts. Ask for informational interviews. Referrals can bypass ATS filtering that rejects 75% of cold applications.

Resume should be updated weekly with new skills, projects, or metrics. Quantify every achievement: 'reduced load time by 40%', 'handled 10K DAU'. ATS systems score resumes — include role-specific keywords.

Take scheduled breaks during job search weeks. One full rest day (Sunday) prevents burnout. Job searching is a full-time job — treat it with professional discipline, including defined working hours and off-hours.
For product management roles, read one product case study per day. Practice product sense questions: design an app for X, how would you improve YouTube, root cause analysis of a metrics drop.

Study company-specific interview patterns before applying. Glassdoor, Blind, and LeetCode Discuss have company-tagged interview reports. Tailor your prep to the specific interview format of each target company.

For data science roles, solve 1 SQL problem daily on StrataScratch or DataLemur, and do one ML model implementation per week. Balance theory (statistics, ML algorithms) with applied coding (pandas, sklearn, PyTorch).
Attend virtual job fairs, company info sessions, and LinkedIn Live events for target employers. These create direct recruiter contact that bypasses standard application black holes.
Schedule a weekly reflection every Friday: which applications are progressing, which topics need more prep, what mock interview feedback to act on. Adjust the coming week's plan based on this review.

Learn about the companies you're applying to: read their engineering blog, recent product launches, business model, and tech stack. Mentioning company-specific knowledge in interviews dramatically improves offer rates.
For front-end roles, build and deploy a portfolio website. Include 3 polished projects with live demos. Understand core web performance concepts: Core Web Vitals, lazy loading, bundle optimization.

Prioritize sleep and mental health during job search. Rejection is statistically inevitable — expect a 5–10% response rate on cold applications. Resilience and consistency over months is what leads to offers.

For backend engineering roles, deeply understand one language (Python, Java, Go) including its concurrency model, runtime, and standard library. Surface-level multi-language knowledge is a red flag for senior interviewers.

Prepare a 60-second personal pitch: who you are, your strongest technical skills, one impressive achievement, and what you're looking for. Use this for recruiter calls, networking events, and LinkedIn summary.

For cloud and DevOps roles, earn at least one certification during job search: AWS Cloud Practitioner, Azure Fundamentals, or GCP Associate. Certifications signal initiative and structured learning to hiring managers.

Practice coding problems on a whiteboard or paper periodically. Some interviews are still whiteboard-based. Being able to write clean code without IDE assistance separates practiced candidates from tool-dependent ones.
